answer:
verdict: migrate to managed k8s (EKS/GKE) asap + use short-term contractor to do the migration. dont bet on 1 replacement hire

why:
- 40 microservices on self-managed k8s w/ 10 engs is way too much infra for team ur size. self-managed = u own control plane, etcd, upgrades, cert rotation, networking — thats a full-time job+ & u just proved u have bus factor of 1
- hiring replacement = 2-4 mo search, $150-200k+, & ur back to same single point of failure. if they quit ur in this exact spot again
- contractors alone = expensive band-aid ($100-200/hr), no long-term ownership, knowledge walks out door again

recommended play:
1. immediate (this wk): audit access — rotate creds/keys the departed eng had, make sure 2+ ppl can deploy & get paged, doc whatever tribal knowledge exists (ask them for paid handoff sessions if exit was amicable)
2. short term: bring in contractor/agency 2-3 mo to keep lights on + execute migration
3. migrate to EKS or GKE (whichever cloud ur already on). managed control plane kills the scariest ops work. go further if u can: fargate/autopilot, managed dbs, managed ingress
4. after migration: make infra shared responsibility across team (IaC via terraform, gitops w/ argocd, runbooks) rather than 1 dedicated devops person. maybe hire a platform-minded SRE later but not as sole owner

caveats:
- if ur on-prem for compliance/data reasons, managed cloud may be off table → then hire 2 (yes 2) infra ppl or accept bus-factor risk
- 40 microservices for 10 engs is also arch smell — worth consolidating services during migration to cut ops load
- migration cost: expect ~2-3 mo eng effort + contractor fees, but cheaper than 1 fte/yr & way less risky
